name: Source Fidelity review for Live Biotherapeutics And Engineered Microbes bundle
summary: The PA53 source, intervention, study, finding, and outlook-refresh records are faithful to PubMed PMID 42235825. The staged records preserve the article title, DOI, publication date, authors, 8-week randomized double-blind placebo-controlled design, n = 75 healthy adults aged 55 to 70 years, PA53 dose of 30 billion CFU/day, maltodextrin placebo, measured well-being, gastrointestinal, sleep, cytokine, and microbiota endpoints, key quantitative results, and the no-conflict statement. The wording is appropriately bounded because it does not convert the short trial into a frailty, disease-progression, mortality, durability, or LEV-relevant result.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged source and study records match PubMed PMID 42235825 for title, journal, DOI, electronic publication date, author list, randomized double-blind placebo-controlled design, n = 75, 55 to 70 year age range, PA53 dose, placebo, and endpoint classes.
findings.why_it_matters: The surveillance bundle depends on PA53 being a real post-baseline older-adult human trial rather than generic probiotic activity or review context.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; keep the staged source and study facts as written.

findings.claim_or_issue: The PubMed abstract reports PA53-group pre/post values for MUNSH, GSRS, BSS, PSQI, IL-6, TNF-alpha, IL-25, and microbiota changes, while noting that PSS had a significant between-group advantage despite a non-significant within-group change.
findings.why_it_matters: The finding should not be read as every endpoint having the same placebo-adjusted statistical interpretation.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; retain the current wording that refers to baseline or placebo-relevant comparisons and keep the low-confidence caveats.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged finding states that the abstract does not list a ClinicalTrials.gov registration ID and that the source reports no author conflicts.
findings.why_it_matters: Registration and conflict details are part of source fidelity and should stay close to the interpretation of a small short-duration trial.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; preserve both caveats in the staged finding.
